attempts.strategy: Blueprint route used affine `finite_order_support_affine` — but it is `private` in WeilDivisor.lean, inaccessible from OcOfD. Pivoted to GLOBAL finiteness of the principal-divisor support `principal g hg` (a Finsupp) on the proper curve; a violator has ord_Q g ≠ 0 ∨ D Q ≠ 0, both sets finite.
attempts.insight: Global principal-divisor finiteness is strictly stronger than the affine bridge and avoids the private cross-file lemma; cost = keeping [IsProper C.hom]. Blueprint prose now diverges (B0).

next_steps: Blueprint prose + \uses edges need rewrite to principal-divisor route (NOTE added).

attempts.strategy: g=0 → U=⊤. Else affine W∋P.point; bad set B finite (B0); each prime-divisor point is closed (coheight=1 ⇒ isClosed_singleton), so Z=⋃_{Q∈B}{Q.point} closed; U:=W⊓Zᶜ. P.point∈U since constraint holds at P.

attempts.insight: P.point∉⊥ extraction needed `have hbot' : (W⊓Zc)=⊥ := hbot; rw[hbot']` — `TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_mem_bot` does not exist; direct membership rewrite works.

Build φ:stalk⟶ModuleCat.of kbar M via colimit.desc of descent cocone with legs Submodule.inclusion; naturality via new helper carrierPresheaf_map_coe. Inj via injective_iff_map_eq_zero; surj via mem_iSup_of_directed (family directed through U⊓V).
